content_en:
Q. 35 What are the things that must be linked with Allah’s Will and what are the things that should not be linked with His Will?

A. It is preferable to link everything in the future to Allah’s Will, according to the Words of Him, the Most High: ‘And never say of anything, ‘I shall do such and such thing tomorrow.’ Except (with the saying), ‘If Allah wills’?!

As for something in the past, it should not be linked to Allah’s Will, unless what is meant by it is to express a hope. So for example, if a person said to you: The month of Ramadan began on Sunday night, Allah Willing, That would not require us to say: Allah Willing, because it is something which has passed and is known. And if someone said to you: I have put on my garment, Allah Willing, and he is wearing it, then it is not fitting to link it to Allah’s Will, because it is something which has past and finished unless he meant by it that the donning of the garment was by Allah’s Will, in that case, there would be no objection to it. And if a person said: I have prayed, Allah Willing, if he meant the action of praying, then this is not fitting, because he has prayed. But if he meant: Allah Willing, the prayer is accepted, then in this case, it would be correct, because he does not know whether his prayer was accepted or not.

Q. 36.What are the categories of Al-Iradah (Intent)?

A. Al-Iradah is divided into two types: 1. Iradah Kawniyyah. 2. Iradah Shari‘ah.

So that which falls under the meaning of Allah’s Will is Iradah Kawniyyah, while that which falls under the meaning of (His) Love (for something) is Iradah Shari‘ah. An example of Iradah Shari‘ah is in the Words of Allah, the Most High:

Speak OF ol Ly a> “Allah wishes to accept your repentance.”\") Because here, the verb;

Fatawa on ‘Agidah (Creed) 135 Baa! G ghd

means loves, it does not mean that He wills it, because if the meaning was: Allah wills that He accept your repentance, He would have accepted the repentance of all of His creatures, and this is something which cannot be, because the majority of the sons of Adam are disbelievers, therefore:

“wishes to accept your repentance.”

means that He loves to accept your repentance and it is not necessary that due to Allah’s Love for something it will happen, because the far-reaching Divine Wisdom might necessitate that it should not happen.

An example of Iradah Kawniyyah is in the Words of Allah, the Most High:

“if Allah's Will is to keep you astray,’"") Allah does not love to make His worshippers go astray, therefore, it is not correct for the meaning to be: ‘If Allah

loved to make you go astray’ rather the meaning is: If Allah wanted to make you go astray.

But it remains for us to say: What is the difference between Tradah Kawniyyah and Iradah Shari‘ah with regard to the occurrence of the matter in question? We say: Regarding the Kawniyyah, that which is wanted must come to be, if Allah wills something to happen, then it must happen:

CENA tow rhe, whe 455 S ALG KEE oh 1 A Cy

“Verily, His Command, when He intends a thing, is only that He

says to it, ‘Be!’ — and it is!”'?!

But as for Iradah Shari‘ah, that which is wanted might come to be and it might not come to be: Allah might desire and love this thing, but it does not occur, because what is loved might take place, and it might not.

If someone said: Does Allah will that sins be committed? We say: He wills it to be, but He does not love that it should be. This is because Iradah Shari‘ah means love, and Allah does not love sins, but He wills them to be, so everything in the heavens and the earth is by Allah’s Will.

Q. 37. What constitutes Ilhad regarding the Names of Allah, the Most High and what are its types?

A. Ithad linguistically means speaking, to be inclined or to deviate, such as in the Words of Allah, the Most High:

“The tongue of the man they refer to is foreign, while this (the Quran) is a clear Arabic tongue.’"")

And from this is the word Lahd,'?! which is so called, because it is inclined to one side of it. I/had cannot be understood without understanding Istigamah,"*! because, as it is said: By their antonyms things are explained. Istigamah in the matter of Allah, the Most High’s Names and Attributes is to accept these Names and Attributes according to their true meaning, in a manner befitting Allah, the Almighty, the All-Powerful, without corrupting the meanings, or negating them, or explaining the how of them, or comparing them with those of the created beings, in accordance with the rule followed by Ahlus-Sunnah wal-Jama‘ah in this matter.

'2] rahd: A niche in the grave in which the deceased is placed, facing towards the Qiblah.

‘31 Istigamah: To be straight.

Fatawa on ‘Agidah (Creed) 137 Bal sg ld

So, if we know what Istiqamah is in this matter, then the opposite of Istigamah is Ilhad. The scholars have mentioned a number of types of I/had regarding the Names and Attributes of Allah, all of which may be covered by saying: It is to deviate away from what it is obligatory to believe regarding them, and that falls into a number of categories, such as:

1, Denying any of the Names or the Attributes proven by them, such as one who denies that the Name: Ar-Rahman (the Most Beneficent) is one of the Names of Allah, the Most High as the people of the Jahiliyyah'") used to do, or to confirm the Names but to deny the Attributes implied by them, as some of the innovators do, saying that Allah, the Most High is Ar-Raheem (the Most Merciful) without having the Attribute of Mercy and As-Sami‘’ (the All-Hearing) without having the Attribute of Hearing.

2. For Allah, the Most Glorified, the Most High to be called by a Name that He has not called Himself.

Here it is I/Jhad because the Names of Allah, the Most Glorified are restricted. So, it is not permissible for anyone to call Allah, the Most High by a name He has not called Himself. To do:so is to say something about Allah without knowledge, and to transgress the Rights of Allah, the Almighty, the All-Powerful. This was done by the philosophers who called the Deity A/-‘Illah Al-Fa’ilah (the Active Cause) and as done by the Christians, who called Allah, by the name ‘the Father’ and the like.

3. To believe that these Names indicate attributes of created beings, and so they declare that they indicate comparison (of Allah with His creation).

. Thisis I/had because one who believes that the Names of Allah, the Most Glorified imply a comparison of Allah with His creation,

"1 Jahiliyyah: The days of ignorance before the Revelation came to the Prophet #8.

Fatawa Arkanul-Islam 138 PEY dIsyt oats

then he has disassociated them from the Attributes which they prove, and deviated thereby from the straight way, and declared the Speech of Allah and the speech of His Messenger 3 to be proof of disbelief. Because comparing Allah with His creation is * disbelief, since it belies the Words of Allah, the Most High:

“There is nothing like Him, and He is the All-Hearer, the AllSeer. ly]

“Do you know of any who is similar to Him?’"?! Nu‘aym bin Hammad AlI-Khuza’i, Al-Bukhari’s Shaikh, may Allah have mercy on them both, said: Whoever compared Allah with His creation, has committed disbelief. And whoever rejected any Attribute Allah described Himself with has

committed an act of disbelief, and there is no comparison in anything by which Allah has described Himself.

4. To derive names for idols from the Names of Allab, the Most High. Such as the derivation of Al-Lat from Al-Ilah (the Deity), Al-Uzza from Al-‘Azeez (the Almighty) and Manat from AlMannan (the Benefactor).

This is I/had because the Names of Allah, the Most High are Unique to Him, so it is not permissible to transfer the meanings which are proven by these Names to any created beings in order to allot him the worship which none has the right for except Allah, the Almighty, the All-Powerful.

These are the forms of I/had regarding the Names of Allah, the Most High.

(1 Ash-Shura 42:11 2) Maryam 19:65

Fatawa on ‘Agidah (Creed) 139 Bide! ig ghd

Q. 38. What are the categories of the things which Allah has attributed to Himself, such as the Face of Allah, the Hand of Allah and such like?

A. The categories of the things which Allah has attributed to Himself are. three:

1. That which is self-existent and its attribution falls under the heading of attributing the created thing to its Creator, and this attribution might be of a general kind, such as in the Words of Allah, the Most High:

Bee dans 62) f yy “Certainly, spacious is My earth.’

And it might be of a particular kind in order to bestow honor on it, such as in the Words of Allah, the Most High:

pi es Sell ugly > “and sanctify My House for those who circumambulate it, and those who stand up (for prayer), and those who bow (submit themselves with humility and obedience to Allah) and make prostration (in prayer),"'?! And His Words:

GZS & a peice “That is the she-camel of Allah! (Do not harm it) and bar it not from having its drink!’""! And this category is created.

2. That which, its existence is brought about by another, such as the Words of Allah, the Most High:

(1 AL‘Ankabut 29:56 1 Ash-Shams 91:13

Fatawa Arkanul-Islam _ 140 PLY! SUT sts

“and a spirit (Ruh) (created) by Him," Attributing this spirit to Allah falls under the heading of attributing the created thing to its Creator in order to honor it. For it is a spirit from among the spirits which Allah has created and it is not a part of Allah, since this spirit was placed in ‘Isa, peace be upon him, and it is a thing which is unconnected to Allah. This category is created also.

3. That it is a clear description in which the governed noun is an Attribute of Allah, and this category is not created, because all of the Attributes of Allah are not created. An example of it is Allah’s Qudrah (Omnipotence) and Allah’s ‘Izzah (Power) and there are many (similar examples) of it in the Quran.

Q. 39. What is the ruling on disputing any of the Names or Attributes of Allah, the Most High?
content_id:
T. 35 Apa saja hal-hal yang harus dikaitkan dengan Kehendak Allah dan apa saja hal-hal yang tidak boleh dikaitkan dengan Kehendak-Nya?

J. Dianjurkan untuk mengaitkan segala sesuatu di masa depan dengan Kehendak Allah, sesuai dengan Firman-Nya Yang Maha Tinggi: 'Dan jangan sekali-kali kamu mengatakan terhadap sesuatu, "Aku pasti melakukan itu besok pagi." Kecuali (dengan mengatakan), "Insya Allah".'

Adapun sesuatu yang telah terjadi di masa lalu, tidak perlu dikaitkan dengan Kehendak Allah, kecuali yang dimaksudkan adalah untuk mengungkapkan harapan. Misalnya, jika seseorang berkata kepadamu: Bulan Ramadan dimulai pada malam Ahad, insya Allah. Hal itu tidak mengharuskan kita mengucapkan: Insya Allah, karena itu adalah sesuatu yang telah berlalu dan diketahui. Dan jika seseorang berkata kepadamu: Saya telah mengenakan pakaian saya, insya Allah, dan dia memang sedang memakainya, maka tidak tepat mengaitkannya dengan Kehendak Allah, karena itu adalah sesuatu yang telah berlalu dan selesai, kecuali jika dia bermaksud bahwa pemakaian pakaian itu adalah atas Kehendak Allah, dalam hal tersebut tidak ada keberatan. Dan jika seseorang berkata: Saya telah salat, insya Allah, jika yang dia maksud adalah perbuatan salatnya, maka ini tidak tepat, karena dia telah salat. Tetapi jika yang dia maksud adalah: Insya Allah, salatnya diterima, maka dalam hal ini ucapannya benar, karena dia tidak mengetahui apakah salatnya diterima atau tidak.

T. 36. Apa saja kategori Al-Iradah (Kehendak)?

J. Al-Iradah terbagi menjadi dua jenis: 1. Iradah Kawniyyah. 2. Iradah Syar'iyyah.

Yang termasuk dalam makna Kehendak Allah adalah Iradah Kawniyyah, sedangkan yang termasuk dalam makna Kecintaan-Nya (terhadap sesuatu) adalah Iradah Syar'iyyah. Contoh Iradah Syar'iyyah terdapat dalam Firman Allah Yang Maha Tinggi:

"Allah hendak menerima taubatmu."(1) Karena di sini, kata kerja tersebut;

Fatawa tentang Aqidah 135 Bab Kedua

bermakna mencintai, bukan bermakna menghendaki, karena jika maknanya adalah: Allah menghendaki untuk menerima taubatmu, niscaya Dia akan menerima taubat seluruh makhluk-Nya, dan ini adalah sesuatu yang tidak mungkin terjadi, karena mayoritas anak Adam adalah orang-orang kafir, oleh karena itu:

"hendak menerima taubatmu."

bermakna bahwa Dia mencintai untuk menerima taubatmu dan tidak harus bahwa karena Kecintaan Allah terhadap sesuatu maka hal itu pasti terjadi, karena Hikmah Ilahiyyah yang jauh jangkauannya mungkin menghendaki agar hal tersebut tidak terjadi.

Contoh Iradah Kawniyyah terdapat dalam Firman Allah Yang Maha Tinggi:

"Jika Allah berkehendak untuk menyesatkanmu."(2) Allah tidak mencintai untuk menyesatkan hamba-hamba-Nya, oleh karena itu, tidak tepat jika maknanya adalah: 'Jika Allah

mencintai untuk menyesatkanmu.' Melainkan maknanya adalah: Jika Allah menghendaki untuk menyesatkanmu.

Namun masih tersisa bagi kita untuk mengatakan: Apa perbedaan antara Iradah Kawniyyah dan Iradah Syar'iyyah berkaitan dengan terjadinya perkara yang dimaksud? Kami katakan: Berkenaan dengan Kawniyyah, apa yang dikehendaki pasti terjadi, jika Allah menghendaki sesuatu terjadi, maka pasti terjadi:

"Sesungguhnya perintah-Nya apabila Dia menghendaki sesuatu hanyalah Dia berkata

kepadanya, 'Jadilah!' — maka jadilah ia!"(3)

Tetapi adapun Iradah Syar'iyyah, apa yang dikehendaki mungkin terjadi dan mungkin juga tidak terjadi: Allah mungkin menginginkan dan mencintai sesuatu, tetapi hal itu tidak terjadi, karena apa yang dicintai mungkin berlangsung, dan mungkin juga tidak.

Jika seseorang bertanya: Apakah Allah menghendaki dosa-dosa dilakukan? Kami katakan: Dia menghendaki terjadinya, tetapi Dia tidak mencintai terjadinya. Ini karena Iradah Syar'iyyah bermakna kecintaan, dan Allah tidak mencintai dosa-dosa, tetapi Dia menghendaki terjadinya, maka segala sesuatu di langit dan di bumi adalah atas Kehendak Allah.

T. 37. Apa yang dimaksud dengan Ilhad berkenaan dengan Nama-Nama Allah Yang Maha Tinggi dan apa saja jenisnya?

J. Ilhad secara bahasa bermakna condong, miring, atau menyimpang, seperti dalam Firman Allah Yang Maha Tinggi:

"Lisan orang yang mereka tuduhkan (bahwa Muhammad belajar) kepadanya adalah bahasa asing, sedangkan ini (Al-Quran) adalah dalam bahasa Arab yang jelas."(4)

Dan dari kata ini pula kata Lahd,(2) yang dinamakan demikian karena ia condong ke salah satu sisinya. Ilhad tidak dapat dipahami tanpa memahami Istiqamah,(3) karena sebagaimana dikatakan: Dengan lawannya segala sesuatu dapat dijelaskan. Istiqamah dalam perkara Nama-Nama dan Sifat-Sifat Allah Yang Maha Tinggi adalah menerima Nama-Nama dan Sifat-Sifat tersebut sesuai dengan makna hakikinya, dengan cara yang layak bagi Allah Yang Maha Perkasa lagi Maha Kuasa, tanpa merusak maknanya, atau menafikannya, atau menjelaskan tata caranya, atau menyerupakannya dengan sifat-sifat makhluk, sesuai dengan kaedah yang diikuti oleh Ahlus Sunnah wal Jama'ah dalam perkara ini.

(2) Lahd: Ceruk di dalam kubur tempat jenazah diletakkan, menghadap ke arah kiblat.

(3) Istiqamah: Lurus, tegak di atas kebenaran.

Fatawa tentang Aqidah 137 Bab Kedua

Maka, jika kita mengetahui apa itu Istiqamah dalam perkara ini, maka lawan dari Istiqamah adalah Ilhad. Para ulama telah menyebutkan sejumlah jenis Ilhad berkenaan dengan Nama-Nama dan Sifat-Sifat Allah, yang semuanya dapat dicakup dengan mengatakan: Yaitu menyimpang dari apa yang wajib diyakini berkenaan dengan Nama-Nama dan Sifat-Sifat tersebut, dan hal itu terbagi menjadi beberapa kategori, seperti:

1. Mengingkari salah satu Nama atau Sifat yang ditetapkan olehnya, seperti orang yang mengingkari bahwa Nama Ar-Rahman (Yang Maha Pengasih) adalah salah satu Nama Allah Yang Maha Tinggi sebagaimana yang dilakukan oleh orang-orang Jahiliyyah,(1) atau menetapkan Nama-Nama tersebut tetapi mengingkari Sifat-Sifat yang ditunjukkan olehnya, sebagaimana yang dilakukan oleh sebagian ahli bid'ah, yang mengatakan bahwa Allah Yang Maha Tinggi adalah Ar-Raheem (Yang Maha Penyayang) tanpa memiliki Sifat Rahmat dan As-Sami' (Yang Maha Mendengar) tanpa memiliki Sifat Mendengar.

2. Menamai Allah Yang Maha Suci lagi Maha Tinggi dengan nama yang tidak Dia namai diri-Nya sendiri dengannya.

Ini adalah Ilhad karena Nama-Nama Allah Yang Maha Suci itu tauqifiyyah (terbatas pada nash). Maka tidak boleh bagi siapa pun menamai Allah Yang Maha Tinggi dengan nama yang tidak Dia namai diri-Nya sendiri dengannya. Melakukan hal itu berarti berkata tentang Allah tanpa ilmu, dan melanggar Hak-Hak Allah Yang Maha Perkasa lagi Maha Kuasa. Ini dilakukan oleh para filosof yang menamakan Tuhan dengan Al-'Illah Al-Fa'ilah (Penyebab Aktif) dan sebagaimana dilakukan oleh orang-orang Kristen yang menamakan Allah dengan nama 'Bapa' dan sejenisnya.

3. Meyakini bahwa Nama-Nama ini menunjukkan sifat-sifat makhluk, sehingga mereka menyatakan bahwa Nama-Nama tersebut menunjukkan penyerupaan (Allah dengan makhluk-Nya).

Ini adalah Ilhad karena siapa yang meyakini bahwa Nama-Nama Allah Yang Maha Suci menunjukkan penyerupaan Allah dengan makhluk-Nya,

(1) Jahiliyyah: Masa kebodohan sebelum wahyu turun kepada Nabi ﷺ.

Fatawa Arkanul-Islam 138

maka dia telah melepaskan Nama-Nama tersebut dari Sifat-Sifat yang ditetapkan olehnya, dan menyimpang dari jalan yang lurus, serta menjadikan Firman Allah dan sabda Rasul-Nya ﷺ sebagai dalil kekufuran. Karena menyerupakan Allah dengan makhluk-Nya adalah kufur, sebab hal itu mendustakan Firman Allah Yang Maha Tinggi:

"Tidak ada sesuatu pun yang serupa dengan-Nya, dan Dia-lah Yang Maha Mendengar lagi Maha Melihat."(1)

"Apakah kamu mengetahui ada sesuatu yang serupa dengan-Nya?"(2) Nu'aym bin Hammad Al-Khuza'i, guru Al-Bukhari, semoga Allah merahmati mereka berdua, berkata: Barangsiapa menyerupakan Allah dengan makhluk-Nya, sungguh dia telah kafir. Dan barangsiapa mengingkari sifat apa pun yang Allah sifati diri-Nya dengannya, sungguh dia telah

melakukan kekufuran, dan tidak ada penyerupaan dalam apa pun yang Allah sifati diri-Nya dengannya.

4. Menurunkan nama-nama untuk berhala dari Nama-Nama Allah Yang Maha Tinggi. Seperti penurunan Al-Lat dari Al-Ilah (Tuhan), Al-Uzza dari Al-'Azeez (Yang Maha Perkasa) dan Manat dari Al-Mannan (Yang Maha Pemberi Karunia).

Ini adalah Ilhad karena Nama-Nama Allah Yang Maha Tinggi adalah khusus bagi-Nya, maka tidak boleh memindahkan makna-makna yang ditetapkan oleh Nama-Nama ini kepada makhluk mana pun untuk memberikan kepadanya ibadah yang tidak berhak diberikan kecuali kepada Allah Yang Maha Perkasa lagi Maha Kuasa.

Inilah bentuk-bentuk Ilhad berkenaan dengan Nama-Nama Allah Yang Maha Tinggi.

(1) Asy-Syura 42:11 (2) Maryam 19:65

Fatawa tentang Aqidah 139 Bab Kedua

T. 38. Apa saja kategori hal-hal yang Allah nisbatkan kepada Diri-Nya, seperti Wajah Allah, Tangan Allah dan sejenisnya?

J. Kategori hal-hal yang Allah nisbatkan kepada Diri-Nya ada tiga:

1. Yang berdiri sendiri dan penisbatannya termasuk dalam kategori penisbatan makhluk kepada Penciptanya, dan penisbatan ini bisa bersifat umum, seperti dalam Firman Allah Yang Maha Tinggi:

Dan bisa juga dari jenis tertentu untuk memberikan kemuliaan padanya, seperti dalam Firman Allah Ta'ala:

pi es Sell ugly > "dan sucikanlah rumah-Ku ini bagi orang-orang yang tawaf, dan orang-orang yang berdiri (untuk salah), dan orang-orang yang ruku' (tunduk dengan kerendahan hati dan ketaatan kepada Allah) dan sujud (dalam salah),"'?! Dan Firman-Nya:

GZS & a peice "Itulah unta betina milik Allah! (Jangan sakiti ia) dan jangan halangi ia dari minumannya!'""! Dan kategori ini adalah makhluk.

2. Sesuatu yang keberadaannya diadakan oleh yang lain, seperti Firman Allah Ta'ala:

(1 Al-'Ankabut 29:56 1 Asy-Syams 91:13

Fatawa Arkanul-Islam _ 140 PLY! SUT sts

"dan ruh (Ruh) (yang diciptakan) oleh-Nya," Penyandaran ruh ini kepada Allah termasuk dalam kategori penyandaran makhluk kepada Penciptanya untuk memuliakannya. Karena ia adalah ruh di antara ruh-ruh yang Allah ciptakan dan ia bukan bagian dari Allah, karena ruh ini ditiupkan ke dalam diri 'Isa 'alaihissalam, dan ia adalah sesuatu yang tidak terhubung dengan Allah. Kategori ini juga makhluk.

3. Bahwa ia adalah sifat yang jelas di mana kata yang diidhafahkan merupakan Sifat Allah, dan kategori ini bukan makhluk, karena semua Sifat Allah tidaklah diciptakan. Contohnya adalah Qudrah (Kemahakuasaan) Allah dan 'Izzah (Kekuasaan) Allah, dan terdapat banyak (contoh serupa) di dalam Al-Quran.

Q. 39. Apa hukum berdebat mengenai salah satu dari Nama-Nama atau Sifat-Sifat Allah Ta'ala?
content_ar: Q. 35 What are the things that must be linked with الله’s الوصية and what are the things that should not be linked with His Will? A. It is preferable to link everything in the future to الله’s Will, according to the Words of Him, the Most High: ‘And never say of anything, ‘I shall do such and such thing tomorrow.’ Except (with the saying), ‘If الله wills’?! As for something in the past, it should not be linked to الله’s Will, unless what is meant by it is to express a hope. So for example, if a person said to you: The month of Ramadan began on Sunday night, الله Willing, That would not require us to say: الله Willing, because it is something which has passed and is known. And if someone said to you: I have put on my garment, الله Willing, and he is wearing it, then it is not fitting to link it to الله’s Will, because it is something which has past and finished unless he meant by it that the donning of the garment was by الله’s Will, in that case, there would be no objection to it. And if a person said: I have prayed, الله Willing, if he meant the action of praying, then this is not fitting, because he has prayed. But if he meant: الله Willing, the الصلاة is accepted, then in this case, it would be correct, because he does not know whether his الصلاة was accepted or not. Q. 36.What are the categories of Al-Iradah (Intent)? A. Al-Iradah is divided into two types: 1. Iradah Kawniyyah. 2. Iradah Shari‘ah. So that which falls under the meaning of الله’s Will is Iradah Kawniyyah, while that which falls under the meaning of (His) Love (for something) is Iradah Shari‘ah. An example of Iradah Shari‘ah is in the Words of الله, the Most High: Speak OF ol Ly a> “الله wishes to accept your التوبة.”\") Because here, the verb; الفتاوى on ‘Agidah (Creed) 135 Baa! G ghd means loves, it does not mean that He wills it, because if the meaning was: الله wills that He accept your التوبة, He would have accepted the التوبة of all of His creatures, and this is something which cannot be, because the majority of the sons of Adam are disbelievers, therefore: “wishes to accept your التوبة.” means that He loves to accept your التوبة and it is not necessary that due to الله’s Love for something it will happen, because the far-reaching Divine Wisdom might necessitate that it should not happen. An example of Iradah Kawniyyah is in the Words of الله, the Most High: “if الله's Will is to keep you astray,’"") الله does not love to make His worshippers go astray, therefore, it is not correct for the meaning to be: ‘If الله loved to make you go astray’ rather the meaning is: If الله wanted to make you go astray. But it remains for us to say: What is the difference between Tradah Kawniyyah and Iradah Shari‘ah with regard to the occurrence of the matter in question? We say: Regarding the Kawniyyah, that which is wanted must come to be, if الله wills something to happen, then it must happen: CENA tow rhe, whe 455 S ALG KEE oh 1 A Cy “Verily, His Command, when He intends a thing, is only that He says to it, ‘Be!’ — and it is!”'?! But as for Iradah Shari‘ah, that which is wanted might come to be and it might not come to be: الله might desire and love this thing, but it does not occur, because what is loved might take place, and it might not. If someone said: Does الله الوصية that sins be committed? We say: He wills it to be, but He does not love that it should be. This is because Iradah Shari‘ah means love, and الله does not love sins, but He wills them to be, so everything in the heavens and the earth is by الله’s Will. Q. 37. What constitutes Ilhad regarding the Names of الله, the Most High and what are its types? A. Ithad linguistically means speaking, to be inclined or to deviate, such as in the Words of الله, the Most High: “The tongue of the man they refer to is foreign, while this (القرآن الكريم) is a clear Arabic tongue.’"") And from this is the word Lahd,'?! which is so called, because it is inclined to one side of it. I/had cannot be understood without understanding Istigamah,"*! because, as it is said: By their antonyms things are explained. Istigamah in the matter of الله, the Most High’s Names and Attributes is to accept these Names and Attributes according to their true meaning, in a manner befitting الله, the Almighty, the All-Powerful, without corrupting the meanings, or negating them, or explaining the how of them, or comparing them with those of the created beings, in accordance with the rule followed by Ahlus-السنة wal-Jama‘ah in this matter. '2] rahd: A niche in the grave in which the deceased is placed, facing towards the القبلة. ‘31 Istigamah: To be straight. الفتاوى on ‘Agidah (Creed) 137 Bal sg ld So, if we know what Istiqamah is in this matter, then the opposite of Istigamah is Ilhad. The العلماء have mentioned a number of types of I/had regarding the Names and Attributes of الله, all of which may be covered by saying: It is to deviate away from what it is واجب to believe regarding them, and that falls into a number of categories, such as: 1, Denying any of the Names or the Attributes proven by them, such as one who denies that the Name: Ar-Rahman (the Most Beneficent) is one of the Names of الله, the Most High as the people of the Jahiliyyah'") used to do, or to confirm the Names but to deny the Attributes implied by them, as some of the innovators do, saying that الله, the Most High is Ar-Raheem (the Most Merciful) without having the Attribute of Mercy and As-Sami‘’ (the All-Hearing) without having the Attribute of Hearing. 2. For الله, the Most Glorified, the Most High to be called by a Name that He has not called Himself. Here it is I/Jhad because the Names of الله, the Most Glorified are restricted. So, it is not مباح for anyone to call الله, the Most High by a name He has not called Himself. To do:so is to say something about الله without knowledge, and to transgress the Rights of الله, the Almighty, the All-Powerful. This was done by the philosophers who called the Deity A/-‘Illah Al-Fa’ilah (the Active Cause) and as done by the Christians, who called الله, by the name ‘the Father’ and the like. 3. To believe that these Names indicate attributes of created beings, and so they declare that they indicate comparison (of الله with His creation). . Thisis I/had because one who believes that the Names of الله, the Most Glorified imply a comparison of الله with His creation, "1 Jahiliyyah: The days of ignorance before the Revelation came to النبي صلى الله عليه وسلم #8. الفتاوى Arkanul-الإسلام 138 PEY dIsyt oats then he has disassociated them from the Attributes which they prove, and deviated thereby from the straight way, and declared the Speech of الله and the speech of His Messenger 3 to be proof of disbelief. Because comparing الله with His creation is * disbelief, since it belies the Words of الله, the Most High: “There is nothing like Him, and He is the All-Hearer, the AllSeer. ly] “Do you know of any who is similar to Him?’"?! Nu‘aym bin Hammad AlI-Khuza’i, البخاري’s Shaikh, رحمهم الله both, said: Whoever compared الله with His creation, has committed disbelief. And whoever rejected any Attribute الله described Himself with has committed an act of disbelief, and there is no comparison in anything by which الله has described Himself. 4. To derive names for idols from the Names of Allab, the Most High. Such as the derivation of Al-Lat from Al-Ilah (the Deity), Al-Uzza from Al-‘Azeez (the Almighty) and Manat from AlMannan (the Benefactor). This is I/had because the Names of الله, the Most High are Unique to Him, so it is not مباح to transfer the meanings which are proven by these Names to any created beings in order to allot him the worship which none has the right for except الله, the Almighty, the All-Powerful. These are the forms of I/had regarding the Names of الله, the Most High. (1 Ash-Shura 42:11 2) Maryam 19:65 الفتاوى on ‘Agidah (Creed) 139 Bide! ig ghd Q. 38. What are the categories of the things which الله has attributed to Himself, such as the Face of الله, the Hand of الله and such like? A. The categories of the things which الله has attributed to Himself are. three: 1. That which is self-existent and its attribution falls under the heading of attributing the created thing to its Creator, and this attribution might be of a general kind, such as in the Words of الله, the Most High: Bee dans 62) f yy “Certainly, spacious is My earth.’ And it might be of a particular kind in order to bestow honor on it, such as in the Words of الله, the Most High: pi es Sell ugly > “and sanctify My House for those who circumambulate it, and those who stand up (for الصلاة), and those who bow (submit themselves with humility and obedience to الله) and make prostration (in الصلاة),"'?! And His Words: GZS & a peice “That is the she-camel of الله! (Do not harm it) and bar it not from having its drink!’""! And this category is created. 2. That which, its existence is brought about by another, such as the Words of الله, the Most High: (1 AL‘Ankabut 29:56 1 Ash-Shams 91:13 الفتاوى Arkanul-الإسلام _ 140 PLY! SUT sts “and a spirit (Ruh) (created) by Him," Attributing this spirit to الله falls under the heading of attributing the created thing to its Creator in order to honor it. For it is a spirit from among the spirits which الله has created and it is not a part of الله, since this spirit was placed in ‘Isa, peace be upon him, and it is a thing which is unconnected to الله. This category is created also. 3. That it is a clear description in which the governed noun is an Attribute of الله, and this category is not created, because all of the Attributes of الله are not created. An example of it is الله’s Qudrah (Omnipotence) and الله’s ‘Izzah (Power) and there are many (similar examples) of it in القرآن الكريم. Q. 39. What is the ruling on disputing any of the Names or Attributes of الله, the Most High?
